meta.title: BMW X3 28i Engine Replacement Cost UK — Compare Quotes
meta.description: Every BMW X3 28i engine — N20 2.0 turbo petrol. F25 generation, real UK costs and reliability. Get free quotes from 20+ vetted BMW specialists today.

mainEntity.name: How much does a BMW X3 28i engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned N20 engine typically costs £2,800–£4,500 supply only. Fitting adds £900–£1,400. Use our calculator for a figure specific to your car.

mainEntity.name: Is the BMW X3 28i engine reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: The N20 is rated ★★★☆☆. It was named to Wards Top 10 Engines in 2012, but the timing chain guide issue is the defining weakness. Pre-2015 cars are at significant risk; 2015+ cars have updated components. Oil leaks and occasional HPFP issues are also reported.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the BMW X3 28i?

acceptedAnswer.text: Timing chain guide failure (especially pre-2015), oil leaks from the valve cover and oil filter housing gaskets, high-pressure fuel pump failure, and turbo wastegate rattle. The timing chain is the most critical — if it fails, the engine is usually scrap.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a BMW X3 28i engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: Depends on vehicle value and build date. Against a well-kept F25 X3 28i worth £5,000–£10,000, a £3,500 recon is 35–70% of the car's value — assess carefully. Post-2015 cars with the updated chain components are more justifiable for replacement.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my BMW X3 28i engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: Listed on the VIN plate and vehicle documentation. The X3 28i uses the N20B20 2.0-litre turbo petrol engine. Tell us your registration date and we'll confirm it for free.

mainEntity.name: Can I repair the N20 timing chain, or does it need replacement?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes — if caught early. A preventative chain and guide replacement (£1,200–£2,500) is far cheaper than a catastrophic chain snap (£2,800–£5,000 engine replacement). At the first sign of cold-start rattle, book an inspection immediately. Post-2015 cars have updated components, but they are not immune to chain wear.

repairBuyOrReplace.h2: BMW X3 28i — Repair, Buy, or Replace?

rows.whenItMakesSense: If caught early; far cheaper than a new engine
Straightforward gasket replacement; catch it early
Preventative — before oil damages the serpentine belt
Specialist replacement; don't let misfires damage the cat
Piston-valve contact means little is left to repair

buyingChecks.title: Buying This X3 28i? Check These First
buyIf: * Post-January 2015 build with the updated timing chain guides
* Documented timing chain replacement history on pre-2015 cars
* Full service history with regular oil changes — the N20 is sensitive to oil quality
* Evidence of valve cover and oil filter housing gasket replacements
avoidIf: * Pre-2015 car with cold-start rattle and no chain history
* Oil leaks with no record of repair — especially from the oil filter housing
* Long crank times or hesitation — potential HPFP issues
* Poor service history on any generation

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The X3 28i's N20 engine is a capable turbo four that offers strong performance and efficiency, but the timing chain guide issue is a well-documented risk that can turn a good car into a money pit. Post-2015 cars are safer, but any example needs a documented service history. Buy on chain history, not on price alone.

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used BMW X3 28i (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: * Cold Start — Listen for any rattle on startup (timing chain guide wear — especially pre-2015)
* Service History — Including which engine era (N20 is the only 28i engine — verify this before purchase)
* Timing Chain History — Has the chain been replaced? Check for updated components on pre-2015 cars
* Oil Leaks — Check for oil spots under the car or a burning oil smell (valve cover or oil filter housing gaskets)
* HPFP Health — Long crank times? Hesitation? HPFP issues are a known occasional failure
* Turbo Health — Any wastegate rattle or loss of boost?
* Engine Warning Light — Have codes been read? Any unresolved timing, fuelling or boost issues?
* MOT History — Check for emissions-related failures
